tweets.content:
BREAKING: U.S. National Detained Near India–Nepal Border

The Sashastra Seema Bal (SSB) has detained a U.S. national near the Sonauli border crossing in Uttar Pradesh after he attempted to enter Nepal illegally, without any valid travel documents.

The individual, who identified himself as Jordan Brown from California, reportedly tried to flee when stopped. Security personnel recovered ₹31,460 in cash and two mobile phones, but no documents confirming his identity.

Brown reportedly claimed to be a former US Navy and Special Forces member, although Indian authorities have not verified those claims. During questioning, he failed to produce a valid passport, visa, or any other travel documents.

The case is under investigation, and authorities have not yet announced any formal charges.

tweets.title: 🚨 Red Fort Blast: Delhi Court Orders Dignified Last Rites for Victims After Months of Investigation 📍Delhi court has permitted the NIA to cremate and dispose of the remains of 11 victims killed in the November 2025 Red Fort terror blast, more than seven months after the attack. 📍The court also allowed the disposal of the remains of suicide bomber Dr. Umer Un Nabi, who died after detonating the explosive laden vehicle. 📍The NIA informed the court that all required forensic examinations had been completed. 📍The court directed that all remains be disposed of with full dignity while respecting the religious beliefs of the victims. It also sought a compliance report from the NIA. 📍The NIA had earlier filed a 7,500 page chargesheet in the Red Fort terror blast case on May 14 as its investigation continues. PTI report
tweets.content:
🚨 Red Fort Blast: Delhi Court Orders Dignified Last Rites for Victims After Months of Investigation

📍Delhi court has permitted the NIA to cremate and dispose of the remains of 11 victims killed in the November 2025 Red Fort terror blast, more than seven months after the attack.

📍The court also allowed the disposal of the remains of suicide bomber Dr. Umer Un Nabi, who died after detonating the explosive laden vehicle.

📍The NIA informed the court that all required forensic examinations had been completed.

📍The court directed that all remains be disposed of with full dignity while respecting the religious beliefs of the victims. It also sought a compliance report from the NIA.

📍The NIA had earlier filed a 7,500 page chargesheet in the Red Fort terror blast case on May 14 as its investigation continues.

PTI report

tweets.content:
"I Fought Three Wars for India, Now I'm Fighting for My Own Land": 92-Year-Old Army Veteran Alleges Fraudulent Sale of Rajasthan Property

A 92-year-old retired Indian Army officer, Captain Chunnilal, who served in the 1962 India-China War and the 1965 & 1971 India-Pakistan wars, has alleged that his agricultural land in Rajasthan's Jaisalmer district was fraudulently mortgaged, transferred and sold using forged documents and an impersonator.
